macro_segments.title: Terumat Ma'aser on Spilled Wine and Oil: The Problem of Gilui and the Solution of Filtering

micro_segments.title: Introduction: The Core Question about Whether Spilled Liquid Can Be Used for Terumat Ma'aser
The Problem of Gilui (Exposure): Snake Poison in Uncovered Water and the Braita's Prohibitions
Rabbi Nechemya's Solution: Using a Mistanena (Sieve) to Filter Out Poison, with Conditions on Covering the Bottom Vessel
The Problem of Pouring with Force: How to Avoid Pushing Poison Through the Filter
Reconciling Rabbi Nechemya with the Case of Demai: Why Tamai Oil Can Be Offered on Tamai Grain

macro_segments.title: Why Oil Cannot Be Used Like Wine: The Distinction Between Loss to the Kohein and Market Value

micro_segments.title: Oil Has High Value for Illumination; Wine for Air Freshening Has Lower Value but Real Market Worth
The Concern of Takalah (Accidental Consumption): Why New Wine Must Be Used Immediately, But Oil Can Be Stored
Why Oil Can Be Kept in a Disgusting Vessel to Prevent Accidental Consumption, But Wine Cannot

macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Beit Shammai and Beit Hillel: Can Tamei Trumah Wine Be Used for Air Freshening?

micro_segments.title: Beit Shammai Requires Spilling; Beit Hillel Allows Air Freshening; Rabbi Yishmael b'Rabbi Yose Offers a Compromise
Why a Third Opinion Cannot Decide Between Established Positions Unless It Recognizes Their Logic

macro_segments.title: Stipulated Payment for Saving Another's Goods: Wine and Honey vs. Donkeys

micro_segments.title: If One Agrees to Reimburse for Spilled Wine When Saving Honey, the Agreement Is Binding
Donkeys: Even Passive Loss Requires Stipulation; Without It, Only Wages Are Owed
If Your Donkey Survives After You Save Another's, You Still Keep Your Stipulated Payment
The Story of Rav Safra and the Lion: Why He Re-Took Possession of His Donkey
If You Attempt But Fail to Save the Donkey, You Get Only Wages, Not the Stipulated Recompense

macro_segments.title: The Cabbage and Plums Case: Payment in Full Even When the Service Becomes Unnecessary

micro_segments.title: Worker Brings Cabbage and Plums at Higher Wage, But Patient Dies or Recovers: Full Payment Still Owed
Why This Case Differs from Failed Rescue: Successfully Completing the Task vs. Attempting Without Success

macro_segments.title: Division of Ransom, Guide Fees, and Donkey-Driver Insurance: Community Customs and Stipulations

micro_segments.title: Ransom by Brigands Divided by Wealth (Kesafim); Guide Services Divided by Both Life and Wealth (Nefashot and Kesafim)
Donkey Drivers' Guild: They Can Stipulate to Replace Lost Donkeys (Except When Lost Through Negligence)
Why Replacement of Donkey (Not Cash) Is Required: Ensuring Shared Interest in Protecting the Herd

macro_segments.title: The Boat and Cargo: Division of Jettison Based on Weight and Seafarers' Custom

micro_segments.title: When a Wave Threatens to Capsize: Cargo Thrown Overboard Is Divided by Weight (Masa), Not Value (Mamon)
Seafarers' Custom and Guild Stipulations: Replacing Boats (with Exceptions for Negligence or Unauthorized Routes)
